title: Before Voices
desc: The Grand Hall is quiet for the moment, which means its crystal ring and resonance bowls can still be prepared without interruption.

steps.text:
Before the muges arrive, the Grand Hall of Muge's Summit resembles an argument waiting for a reason. The chamber is carved from one vast body of crystalline stone, and even in silence it hums very faintly, as if remembering older conversations that never fully left. Pale light from the corridor glow beds travels into the crystal walls and returns in softer bands, crossing the central floor ring and the thirteen speaker stations with the slow inevitability of water finding its level. For a few minutes, before anyone starts insisting on theory, the hall is almost serene.

The chamber keeper sets down a tray of cloths beside the central ring. "Polish the crystal floor band if you want the lower work," she says. "Or fill and place the resonance bowls around the speaker circle if you prefer the stations. Once the muges arrive, this room will stop being beautiful and return to being useful."

options.label: Polish the central crystal band across the floor
Fill and place the resonance bowls at the speaker stations

steps.text:
The floor band is worn smooth by years of careful pacing, sudden objections, and one suspects, a great deal of formal indignation. Dust has dimmed the crystal just enough to steal the depth from it. You wipe the ring in long arcs, clearing the haze until the internal lines of the stone begin showing again, bright and intricate under the hall's cold light. As the band clears, the reflected glow from the walls gathers in it more strongly, turning the circle into a faint luminous track around the center of the chamber.

When you step back, the hall looks larger than it did before, not because anything changed in scale but because the crystal has resumed answering the light properly. The keeper studies the ring and seems satisfied by the calm it imposes. "Better. It is useful to remind the muges that the stone was resonant before any of them started arguing at it."

options.label: Fold the cloths and leave the crystal band shining

steps.text:
The resonance bowls are shallow crystal cups set at each of the thirteen stations, each one holding just enough water to show vibration when a note, phrase, or counterpoint starts going wrong. You fill them in measured amounts, carry them around the circle, and set each bowl into its notch so the surface sits level and ready. Once placed, the ring of still water around the chamber looks almost ceremonial until you remember its real purpose is to make disagreement visible.

The keeper walks the circle behind you, checking each meniscus against the rim. All thirteen hold steady, reflecting the chamber ceiling and nothing else. For now, at least, the hall is balanced. "Good," she says. "Let the first disturbance come from a voice and not from our preparation."

options.label: Set the water jug aside and leave the stations prepared
